result.stdout:
Description:
  Compiles, runs and analyses Yarn code.

Usage:
  ysc [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  compile <inputs>                       Compiles Yarn scripts.
  list-sources <yarnproject>             Lists Yarn sources for a Yarn project.
  run <inputs>                           Runs Yarn scripts in an interactive manner
  print-tree <input>                     Parses a Yarn script and produces a human-readable syntax tree.
  print-tokens <input>                   Parses a Yarn script and produces list of parsed tokens.
  tag <inputs>                           Tags a Yarn script with localisation line IDs
  extract <inputs>                       Extracts strings from the provided files
  graph <inputs>                         Exports a graph view of the dialogue
  version                                Show version info
  browse-binary <compiledInput>          Browses some of the common data inside of a compiled yarn program
  create-proj <project-name>             Creates a yarn project file with default values
  dump-code <inputs>                     Compiles the specified input, and prints the disassembled code.
  generate-definitions <inputDirectory>  Generates YSLS files for your project.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Compiles, runs and analyses Yarn code.

Usage:
  ysc [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  compile <inputs>                       Compiles Yarn scripts.
  list-sources <yarnproject>             Lists Yarn sources for a Yarn project.
  run <inputs>                           Runs Yarn scripts in an interactive manner
  print-tree <input>                     Parses a Yarn script and produces a human-readable syntax tree.
  print-tokens <input>                   Parses a Yarn script and produces list of parsed tokens.
  tag <inputs>                           Tags a Yarn script with localisation line IDs
  extract <inputs>                       Extracts strings from the provided files
  graph <inputs>                         Exports a graph view of the dialogue
  version                                Show version info
  browse-binary <compiledInput>          Browses some of the common data inside of a compiled yarn program
  create-proj <project-name>             Creates a yarn project file with default values
  dump-code <inputs>                     Compiles the specified input, and prints the disassembled code.
  generate-definitions <inputDirectory>  Generates YSLS files for your project.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Browses some of the common data inside of a compiled yarn program

Usage:
  ysc browse-binary <compiledInput> [options]

Arguments:
  <compiledInput>  The .yarnc file you want to browse

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Compiles Yarn scripts.

Usage:
  ysc compile <inputs>... [options]

Arguments:
  <inputs>  The .yarnproject file to compile, or a collection of .yarn files to compile.

Options:
  -o, --output-directory <output-directory>                      Output directory (default: current directory) [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-yarnspinner.console-3.2.1-ca8126ccb6d848f3ad2a1b434f187666]
  -n, --output-name <output-name>                                Output name to use for the files (default: Output) [default: Output]
  -t, --output-string-table-name <output-string-table-name>      Output string table filename (default: {name}-Lines.csv
  -m, --output-metadata-table-name <output-metadata-table-name>  Output metadata table filename (default: {name}-Metadata.csv
  --stdout                                                       Output machine-readable compilation result to stdout instead of to files
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a yarn project file with default values

Usage:
  ysc create-proj <project-name> [options]

Arguments:
  <project-name>  The project name

Options:
  -u, --unity-exclusion  Set the excludeFiles value to ignore folders with trailing ~ (default: false)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

Arguments:

Options:
  -p, --allow-preview-features  Allow using in-development compiler features.
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Extracts strings from the provided files

Usage:
  ysc extract <inputs>... [options]

Arguments:
  <inputs>  The .yarnproject file indicating Yarn files to extract strings from, or a collection of .yarn files.

Options:
  -f, --format <csv|xlsx>        The export file format for the extracted strings, defaults to csv [default: csv]
  --columns <columns>            The desired columns in the exported table of strings. [default: character|text|id]
  --default-name <default-name>  The default character name to use. Defaults to none.
  -o, --output <output>          File location for saving the extracted strings. Defaults to a file named lines in the current directory
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --default-name <default-name>  The default character name to use. Defaults to none.
  -o, --output <output>          File location for saving the extracted strings. Defaults to a file named lines in the current directory
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates YSLS files for your project.

Usage:
  ysc generate-definitions <inputDirectory> [options]

Arguments:
  <inputDirectory>  The directory of the project containing your implementation files

Options:
  -o, --output-directory <output-directory>                Output directory (default: current directory) [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-yarnspinner.console-3.2.1-ca8126ccb6d848f3ad2a1b434f187666]
  -e, --project-type <Godot-csharp|Godot-gd|Unity|Unreal>  The engine this project uses. Used to determine the engine specific variations of the generated YSLS file. [default: Unity]
  -?, -h, --help                                           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Exports a graph view of the dialogue

Usage:
  ysc graph <inputs>... [options]

Arguments:
  <inputs>  The .yarnproject file indicating Yarn files to export a graph of, or a collection of .yarn files.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>       File location for saving the graph. Defaults to a file named dialogue in the current directory
  -f, --format <dot|mermaid>  The graph format, defaults to dot [default: dot]
  -c, --clustering            Generate a graph with clustering subgraphs (default: false)
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Parses a Yarn script and produces a human-readable syntax tree.

Usage:
  ysc print-tree <input>... [options]

Arguments:
  <input>  The .yarnproject file indicating files to generate a parse tree from, or a collection of .yarn files.

Options:
  -o, --output-directory <output-directory>  Output directory (default: current directory) [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-yarnspinner.console-3.2.1-ca8126ccb6d848f3ad2a1b434f187666]

  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Runs Yarn scripts in an interactive manner

Usage:
  ysc run <inputs>... [options]

Arguments:
  <inputs>  The .yarnproject file to run, or a collection of .yarn files to run. One of the specified files must contain the start node.

Options:
  -s, --start-node <start-node>  Name of the node to start running [default: Start]
  -a, --auto-advance             Auto-advance regular dialogue lines
  -p, --allow-preview-features   Allow using in-development compiler features.
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Runs Yarn scripts in an interactive manner

Usage:
  ysc run <inputs>... [options]

Arguments:
  <inputs>  The .yarnproject file to run, or a collection of .yarn files to run. One of the specified files must contain the start node.

Options:
  -s, --start-node <start-node>  Name of the node to start running [default: Start]
  -a, --auto-advance             Auto-advance regular dialogue lines
  -p, --allow-preview-features   Allow using in-development compiler features.
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Tags a Yarn script with localisation line IDs

Usage:
  ysc tag <inputs>... [options]

Arguments:
  <inputs>  The .yarnproject file indicating files to add tags to, or a collection of .yarn files

Options:
  -o, --output-directory <output-directory>  Output directory to write the newly tagged files (default: override the input files)
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information
